name: Allied Cash Advance
description_short: Allied Cash Advance Thatcher, Arizona — Allied Cash Advance offers installment loans, cash advances, and prepaid cards at physical locations. Fast fundi...
description_long:
Allied Cash Advance is a physical storefront lender operating multiple locations, including the Mesa, Arizona branch at 2706 East University Drive. The company specializes in providing rapid access to cash through installment loans and related financial products for consumers who need funds quickly. The Mesa location maintains extended hours (10am-6pm weekdays, 10am-2pm Saturday) and serves as both an application and funding center.

The company's primary offerings include unsecured installment loans marketed as alternatives to payday loans, with fixed repayment schedules. They also offer Netspend Prepaid Mastercard products and refinancing/reloading services for existing customers. Funding timelines are competitive: same-day funding (as fast as 30 minutes with debit card) for in-store applications, or next business day for online applications. The application process requires proof of income, valid ID, an active checking account (30+ days old), and a working phone number.

Allied Cash Advance distinguishes itself through high customer satisfaction ratings (4.9/5 stars on Google with 377 reviews for the Mesa location) and emphasis on staff professionalism. The company markets installment loans as structured alternatives to traditional payday loans, with fixed repayment terms rather than lump-sum balloon payments. They explicitly state they consider applicants with bad or no credit, making credit flexibility a key positioning point.

The company operates transparently about limitations: they will not advise on credit score impact, cannot guarantee approval for those with poor credit history, and require active bank accounts for fund transfers. The business model depends on loan fees and interest charges, which are not disclosed on the website. This is a direct lender (not a broker), meaning customers work with the company directly rather than being referred elsewhere.
pros: Same-day funding available (as fast as 30 minutes) for in-store applicants using debit card
Extended hours including Saturday (10am-2pm) for convenience
High customer satisfaction ratings (4.9/5 stars, 377 Google reviews)
Considers applicants with bad or no credit history
Installment loans with fixed repayment schedules (not balloon payments like payday loans)
Multiple application channels: online, in-store, and phone (where available)
cons: Interest rates and fees not disclosed on website; pricing information requires direct inquiry
Requires 30-day-old active checking account (excludes those without established bank history)
Cannot advise on credit score impact, creating uncertainty for applicants
Limited to specific service areas (website shows Arizona locations only in sample)
High-cost credit product with embedded interest and fees not transparent upfront
best_for: Consumers needing cash within hours and willing to pay premium rates for speed
Those with active checking accounts who need small-to-medium short-term loans ($100-$1K typical range)
Applicants with poor or no credit history unable to qualify for bank loans
Borrowers preferring fixed installment repayment over payday loan balloon structures
diagnosis: Allied Cash Advance is best suited for consumers who need emergency cash within 24 hours and have an active checking account, even with poor credit. The primary caveat is that this is a high-cost credit product where exact rates and fees are not disclosed online—customers must apply or call to learn actual costs, and the fast-funding convenience comes at a premium price premium.
services: Installment loans (unsecured personal loans with fixed repayment schedules)
Netspend Prepaid Mastercard reloadable debit card
Retail remote refinancing for existing customers
Retail remote reloans for existing customers
